result.summary: Optimizely exposes multiple developer surfaces including Web Experimentation REST APIs, Optimizely Data Platform REST and GraphQL APIs, an Optimizely Graph GraphQL API, the Experimentation Data credential-exchange API, and the `oevents` CLI.
result.description: Optimizely provides digital experience, experimentation, and customer data products for managing content, running experiments, and activating customer data. Its product documentation covers separate APIs for web experimentation, Optimizely Graph, data platform workflows, and experimentation data export.

web_pat.label: Web Experimentation personal access token
web_pat.setup: Create a Personal Access Token in Optimizely Web Experimentation by following the instructions in [Personal access token](https://docs.developers.optimizely.com/web-experimentation/docs/personal-access-token). Use that token as a bearer token in the `Authorization` header for the Web Experimentation REST API and for the Experimentation Data Authentication API when exchanging for temporary export credentials.

web_oauth_app.label: Web Experimentation OAuth client
web_oauth_app.setup: Register an OAuth application in Optimizely Web Experimentation using the steps in [Registration](https://docs.developers.optimizely.com/web-experimentation/docs/registration). After registration, use the OAuth 2.0 flow documented under [OAuth 2.0](https://docs.developers.optimizely.com/web-experimentation/docs/oauth-20) to obtain bearer access tokens for the REST API.

odp_api_key.label: Optimizely Data Platform private API key
odp_api_key.setup: Generate or locate your Optimizely Data Platform private API key from your ODP account as described in [Authentication](https://docs.developers.optimizely.com/optimizely-data-platform/reference/authentication). The docs and examples use this key in the `x-api-key` header for ODP REST and GraphQL requests.
